summary: Backend-only pytest suite for Temple penalty engine. 7/10 tests passed. Login, penalty summary, recompute (idempotent), pending list, penalty math (25 * missed_months), rate stability, and legacy /api/user/admins_view/ all PASS against external preview URL. 3 failures are the SAME root cause: missing Authorization header returns HTTP 403 (DRF AuthenticationFailed default) instead of the 401 required by the spec.

critical.issue: Returns 403 'Unauthenticated!' instead of 401 when Authorization header is missing. Root cause: token_app.views.token_checking raises rest_framework.exceptions.AuthenticationFailed, which DRF maps to 403 by default (no authenticator on @api_view => no WWW-Authenticate => 403). The _require_auth helper in penalty_views.py never gets a chance to return its 401 Response because the exception is raised first.

action_items: Fix 401-vs-403: In /app/backend/amount/penalty_views.py::_require_auth, wrap token_checking(request) in try/except AuthenticationFailed and return Response({'message':'Not Authorized'}, status=401). Alternative: change token_checking to return None on missing/invalid header instead of raising. Required because the review spec explicitly demands 401 for unauthenticated calls to all three penalty endpoints.
Optional polish: token_checking() uses bare 'except:' (line 17) which swallows real errors and returns 417 - should at least log and narrow the except clause.
critical_code_review_comments: amount/penalty_views.py _require_auth: business logic of catching auth errors is missing - relies on token_checking which raises AuthenticationFailed; this contradicts the helper's contract of returning (None, Response 401).
token_app/views.py token_checking returns mixed types (User instance OR DRF Response on some error paths, raises on others). Inconsistent contract makes downstream views hard to reason about.
penalty_engine.recompute_all() calls qs.count() AFTER iterating qs - acceptable but evaluates the query twice; consider counting len(items_iterated) for efficiency on large data sets.
penalty_views.pending_penalty_list calls _due_date(p) twice per row inside _serialize_item; minor N+1-ish overhead.
No HOT-RELOAD concern: only one ManagementDetails row is used (.first()) - works for current data but multi-tenant safety should be considered.

success_rate.backend: 70% (7/10 - all 3 failures are the same 401-vs-403 issue)

test_credentials: admin@gmail.com / Admin@123 (works; jwt returned in 'jwt' field, sent raw without 'Bearer ' prefix)
seed_data_creation: None - used existing MySQL temple DB (884 unpaid PeoplesAmountDetails rows). Verified total_pending_penalty > 0 and that recompute is idempotent (updated=0 on second call).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 4 core penalty endpoints work and math is correct (rate=25.0, penalty_amount == 25*missed_months for un-paid rows, idempotent recompute, legacy /api/user/admins_view/ returns 200). Only remaining gap is HTTP status code 401 for missing Authorization header - currently 403. After main agent's fix, re-run: pytest /app/backend/tests/test_penalty_api.py -v . Auth header format is raw JWT (no 'Bearer '). Use external URL https://fd32960a-5552-41c8-bee2-e9a5572be60a.preview.emergentagent.com.
rca of the issue: Reproduction: curl -i https://<host>/api/penalty/summary/  (no Authorization header). Expected: 401. Actual: 403 with body {"detail":"Unauthenticated!"}. RCA: token_app.views.token_checking raises rest_framework.exceptions.AuthenticationFailed when the Authorization header is missing. DRF's default exception handler converts AuthenticationFailed to 401 ONLY when an authenticator is present that supplies a WWW-Authenticate challenge; otherwise it returns 403 (see rest_framework/views.py exception_handler + NotAuthenticated logic). Since @api_view([...]) without authentication_classes has no authenticator, DRF falls back to 403. Mitigation (one of): (a) catch AuthenticationFailed in _require_auth and return Response({'message':'Not Authorized'}, status=401); (b) change token_checking to return None instead of raising on missing header; (c) attach authentication_classes=[] alongside a custom one supplying authenticate_header. Option (a) is the smallest-blast-radius fix.
